originalTitle: New outbreak of Ebola kills 65 in eastern DR Congo
title: Superheroes Helping Friends Stay Healthy in DR Congo!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends all across Africa! I'm your special news reporter, here to tell you about important things happening in our big, beautiful continent. Today, we're going to talk about staying healthy and helping our friends in a place called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. Sometimes, tiny, tiny things called germs can make people sick, but lots of grown-ups are working super hard to help everyone get better.

What's a Sneaky Germ Called Ebola?

Imagine a tiny, invisible little bug, much smaller than an ant, that can make people feel very, very poorly. This bug has a big name: Ebola. When someone gets Ebola, they might feel very hot, like they have a fever, or have a tummy ache. It's not like a normal sniffle or cough. It's a special kind of sickness that needs special care from doctors and nurses. It's important to remember that most germs don't make us this sick, and you can't see them! But doctors know how to find them and help. Right now, some people in DR Congo have this sickness, and a few people have become very unwell. We hear that about 65 people have become very sick, and about 246 people have caught this tricky germ. But don't worry, many people are working hard to help. * **Did you know?** Germs are so small, you need a very special microscope to see them! They can be anywhere, on your toys, your hands, or even in the air! That's why we wash our hands a lot!

Where is This Happening?

Now, let's talk about where this is happening. The Democratic Republic of Congo is a big country right here in Africa! It's famous for its big forests and amazing animals, like gorillas and okapi. This sickness is mostly in the eastern part of DR Congo. Imagine a big country, and just one part of it is having this trouble. And guess what? There's another country close by called Uganda, and one person there also got sick. But the doctors and helpers in Uganda quickly found out and are doing everything to stop the germ from spreading to more people. This shows how quickly our African health heroes work together across different countries! * **Did you know?** Africa is the second-largest continent in the world, with 54 amazing countries! DR Congo and Uganda are neighbors, just like you might have a neighbor next door!

How Do We Stay Safe from Germs?

The best way to fight any sneaky germ, including Ebola, is to keep ourselves clean and healthy! Think of your hands as superhero tools. After playing outside, before eating, and after using the toilet, what do you do? You wash your hands with soap and water! Well, washing your hands is like making a bubbly shield against germs. It washes them all away! Also, it's very important not to touch people who are sick, especially if they have a fever or tummy ache. If you see someone who looks unwell, tell a grown-up right away. Grown-ups know how to help safely. * **Did you know?** When you wash your hands properly, you should sing "Happy Birthday" twice to make sure you've scrubbed long enough! It's a fun way to fight germs!

Superheroes in White Coats: Doctors and Nurses

Our amazing doctors and nurses are like real-life superheroes! When someone gets sick with Ebola, these brave men and women put on special gear. They wear big gloves, masks, and sometimes full suits that cover them from head to toe. This keeps them safe while they help the sick people. They give people medicine to help them feel better, make sure they drink enough water, and check on them every day. They are working very, very hard day and night in DR Congo and Uganda to make sure everyone gets the best care. They are so brave and kind, always putting others first. * **Did you know?** These special suits are called Personal Protective Equipment, or PPE! They are like a superhero uniform that stops germs from getting to the doctors and nurses.

What Else Are People Doing?

It's not just doctors and nurses! Many other grown-ups are helping too. Health agencies, which are big groups of smart people, are sending help and resources to DR Congo and Uganda. They are teaching people about the sickness, how to stay safe, and how to look after each other. They are also working to find out exactly where the sickness came from and how it is spreading. This helps them to put a stop to it even faster. Everyone is working together, like a big team, to beat this germ and make sure everyone in our African family stays healthy and safe. * **Did you know?** Scientists and doctors from all over the world often work together to solve big health problems, sharing their knowledge and help! Teamwork makes the dream work!

We Can All Help!

Even though you are small, you can still be a big helper! The most important thing you can do is to always, always wash your hands. Remember to use soap and water, and scrub them well. Tell your friends and family how important it is too! If you ever feel sick, even a little bit, like a cough or a tummy ache, tell a grown-up straight away. Your parents, teachers, or guardians will know what to do. Staying home when you're sick helps to stop your germs from spreading to others. Being a good helper means looking after yourself and others. * **Did you know?** Eating yummy fruits and vegetables and getting enough sleep helps your body stay strong and ready to fight off germs! Be a strong, healthy kid!

Looking After Each Other

It's important to remember that while this sickness is serious, many kind and clever people are working hard to make things better. We should always be kind to everyone, especially those who might be sick or scared. We are all part of one big African family, and we look out for each other. Let's send our happy thoughts and well wishes to our friends in DR Congo and Uganda. We know that with everyone working together, staying clean, and listening to our health heroes, we can overcome this challenge. Stay healthy, my wonderful friends, and keep smiling!
summary: Doctors and health helpers are working very hard in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda to stop a sickness called Ebola from making more people unwell, and we can all help by keeping our hands clean and staying safe.
whyMatters: This matters because everyone deserves to be healthy and safe, and by learning about germs like Ebola, we can understand how important it is to keep ourselves clean and listen to grown-ups who are helping people get better.

originalTitle: Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years in rare corruption verdict
title: Let's Talk About Being Fair and Honest!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! It’s your friendly news reporter here, ready to share some important news in a super easy way. Today, we're going to talk about being fair and making sure everyone gets what they need. It’s a bit like sharing your toys or your delicious snacks with your friends!

A Very Important Job

There was a man in Nigeria, a big country in Africa, named Mr. Saleh Mamman. He used to have a very, very important job. Imagine someone who helps make sure all your lights work, your TV can turn on, and your mom or dad can charge their phone. That’s what he did! His special job was called being the 'Power Minister'. A minister is like a very special helper for the whole country. This helper makes big decisions to make life better for everyone. Mr. Mamman's job was to help make sure everyone had electricity. That’s the power that makes things light up and work! Did you know? Electricity is super important! It helps us read books at night, keeps our food cold in the fridge, and even powers the hospitals where doctors help sick people feel better. Imagine if you couldn't turn on your lights to read your favourite storybook! That's why Mr. Mamman's job was so, so big and important.

What Went Wrong?

Now, sometimes, even people with very important jobs can make big mistakes. Mr. Mamman was found to have done something called 'corruption'. That's a big word, but let's make it simple. Imagine your teacher gives you money to buy colourful new crayons for the whole class. But instead of buying the crayons for everyone, you take some of that money and buy yourself a big, yummy ice cream that’s just for you! And then the rest of the class doesn't get enough crayons. That wouldn’t be fair, right? Well, corruption is a bit like that. It means someone who has a special job uses their power to take money or things that belong to everyone, and they keep it for themselves or give it to their friends unfairly. This money should have been used to build new schools, or make sure even more homes had electricity, or buy medicines for hospitals. When someone is corrupt, they stop good things from happening for everyone else.

The Judges and The Rules

When someone does something unfair or wrong, we have special people called judges. Judges work in a place called a court. A court is like a big classroom where everyone talks about what happened, and the judges listen very carefully to all sides. Their job is to make sure everyone follows the rules and that things are fair for everyone. Just like your teacher makes sure everyone follows the classroom rules! After looking at all the information, the judges said that Mr. Mamman did something wrong. They said he was not fair, and he broke the rules about using money for the country. This made them very, very sad because his job was to help people, not to be unfair.

A Very Big Consequence

Because what he did was a very serious mistake, the judges gave him a big consequence. They said he would have to go away for a very, very long time – 75 years! Can you imagine how long 75 years is? It’s much, much longer than you’ve been alive. It’s even longer than your parents or grandparents have lived! It's a very big number, and it shows how serious being unfair with the country's money is. This big punishment is to show everyone that being fair and honest is super important. It also tells people that if they try to be unfair and take what’s not theirs, there will be big consequences. Did you know? Nigeria is the most populous country in Africa! That means many, many people live there. And all those people need electricity and fairness.

Where Is He?

Now, here’s a puzzling part of the story. Even though the judges said Mr. Mamman should go away for a long time, no one knows where he is! It's like playing hide-and-seek, but it's not a game. The people who help keep the country safe, like the police, are looking for him. They need to find him so that he can face the consequences the judges decided. It’s important that everyone, no matter how important their job, follows the rules. When someone tries to hide, it makes it harder for justice to be done. Justice means making things right again.

Why Does This Matter to You?

You might think, “I’m just a kid, why does this matter to me?” But it matters a lot! When people in charge are honest and fair, it means there’s more money and resources for good things. It means more schools can be built, more books can be bought for your libraries, more doctors can help sick children, and yes, more homes can have the electricity they need to light up their lives. When people are corrupt, it means less for everyone. It's like that candy jar again – if one person takes too much, there's less for you and your friends. Did you know? Sharing and being honest are some of the best ways to make friends and have a happy school! The same goes for countries!

Let’s Be Fair and Honest!

So, my friends, this news story reminds us all about how important it is to be fair, to be honest, and to always follow the rules. Whether you’re sharing your toys, playing a game, or growing up to have a big job like a minister, being honest makes the world a better place for everyone. Let’s all try our best to be fair every single day! We want a bright and fair future for all of Africa! That’s all for today from your friendly reporter. See you next time!
summary: A former Power Minister in Nigeria, Mr. Saleh Mamman, was found to have been unfair and took money that was not his (corruption), and a judge said he must go away for a very long time, but no one knows where he is right now.
whyMatters: This news is important because it teaches us that everyone, even people with big jobs, must be honest and fair. When people are not fair, it hurts everyone by taking away money that could be used for good things like schools and electricity, and it shows that big mistakes have big consequences, even if someone tries to hide.

originalTitle: Activists hang Palestinian flag on the Eiffel Tower for Nakba Day
title: A Big Flag on a Super Tall Tower!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here to tell you a super interesting story from far, far away. Today, we're going to talk about a very, very tall tower and a special flag that went up high on it. Get ready for an adventure in words!

A Big Tower in France!

Imagine a building that is so, so tall, it almost touches the clouds! Taller than any tree or house you have ever seen. This super tall building is called the Eiffel Tower. It is one of the most famous places in the whole wide world! It lives in a country called France, in a big, busy city named Paris. Many people visit the Eiffel Tower every single day because it is so special and beautiful. It's made of lots and lots of iron, strong and grand. Did you know? The Eiffel Tower is almost 330 meters tall! That’s like stacking almost 100 big school buses on top of each other! It's truly a giant in the city.

What Happened?

One sunny day, something very special and brave happened at this super tall tower. Some people wanted to share an important message with everyone. So, they decided to do something that would make everyone look up and listen! These people climbed up a little bit of the Eiffel Tower. Not all the way to the very, very top, but high enough for lots and lots of people to see what they were doing. It was a very brave thing to do, like climbing a tall tree to get a kite that got stuck! When they were high up, they carefully hung a special piece of cloth. This cloth had beautiful colors and shapes on it. What do we call a special piece of cloth with colors and shapes that tells us about a country or a group of people? That’s right, it was a flag!

A Flag and Its Colors

The flag they hung was the flag for a place called Palestine. Every country has a flag, like our own amazing countries in Africa! Flags are like big colourful pictures that tell us about a place or its people. They have special colours and designs that mean different things. The Palestinian flag has colors like black, white, green, and red. These colors are very important to the people of Palestine. When people see this flag, they know it represents Palestine and its people. It's a way of saying, 'This is us, and we are here!' Did you know? Some flags have stars, some have animals, and some have special shapes. Each part of a flag usually has a special meaning to the country it belongs to. It's like a secret story told in colours!

What is Nakba Day? (Very Simple Explanation)

The people hung the Palestinian flag on the Eiffel Tower because it was a very special and important day for the people of Palestine. This day is called Nakba Day. Nakba Day is a day when people from Palestine remember sad things that happened to their families and their homes a long, long time ago. It's like a day for remembering an old, sad story that their grandmas and grandpas might have told them. It's important to remember sad things, not to be sad forever, but so that we can learn from them and help each other. Just like when you remember a special story your family shares, good or sad, it helps you understand your family better. On Nakba Day, people want to make sure that others around the world remember what happened to the people of Palestine. They want to show that they care and remember their history, even the hard parts.

Who are these Special People?

The people who climbed the Eiffel Tower with the flag are part of a group called 'Extinction Rebellion'. That's a long name, isn't it? Let's call them 'The Caring Climbers' for our story! 'The Caring Climbers' are people who care a lot about important things in the world. They want to make sure that everyone listens to their messages and knows about things that are happening. Sometimes, they do big and noticeable things to get people's attention, like putting a flag on a famous tower! They are like big brothers and sisters who try to make sure everyone hears about something important. They want the world to be a fair and kind place for everyone. Did you know? There are many groups of people all over the world who care about making things better. They might care about animals, or our planet, or making sure everyone has enough food. 'The Caring Climbers' want to help people.

Why Did They Do This?

'The Caring Climbers' wanted to show love and support for the people of Palestine on Nakba Day. When you show support, it means you are telling someone, 'I am thinking of you, and I care about what happens to you.' They knew that by putting the Palestinian flag on a famous place like the Eiffel Tower, many, many people would see it. People in France, and people all over the world, would see the flag and remember Palestine. It's like when you draw a beautiful picture for a friend when they are feeling sad, or when you cheer for your favourite team in a game. You are showing them you care and you are with them.

Showing We Care

This story reminds us that even if people live very far away from each other, we can still show we care. People like 'The Caring Climbers' want to make sure everyone feels remembered and supported. This is a very important part of being a good friend to everyone in our big, wide world. When we see someone asking for help or showing that they care, it helps us all to think about others. It makes us remember that we are all part of one big family, living on one amazing planet. Did you know? When people show support, it can make others feel less alone and stronger. Even a small act of kindness, like a smile or a kind word, can make a big difference!

Our World, Our Friends

So, from the Eiffel Tower in France, a flag flew high to remind everyone about Palestine and Nakba Day. It was a brave act by 'The Caring Climbers' to show the world that remembering history and supporting others is very important. And that's the exciting news from your friendly reporter in Africa today! Remember to always be kind and care for your friends, near and far. Until next time, keep exploring and learning, amazing kids!

originalTitle: Trump says 20-year nuclear programme suspension by Iran would be enough
title: Big News About Special Power and Staying Safe!
content:
Hello, amazing friends all across Africa! I'm your special kid-friendly news reporter, and I'm super excited to share some important news with you today. It's about big leaders from countries far away talking about special power!

What's This Big News About?

Imagine two big countries, far, far away from us. One country has a special leader called President Trump. He leads a country called the United States of America. It's a very big country! The other country is called Iran, and it also has its own important leaders. Sometimes, big countries talk to each other to make sure everyone stays safe and happy in the world. This news is about one of those important talks.

Special Power: What is it?

Now, let's talk about something called "nuclear power." Wow, that's a big word, right? Don't worry, I'll explain it simply! Think of it like this: there's a super-duper strong kind of energy. It's so strong it can light up whole cities! It can make electricity for your homes, your schools, and all the hospitals. It's like magic power for light and machines! But this super-duper strong energy needs to be handled very, very carefully. If it's not used correctly, it can also be used to make very, very big explosions. Like super big firecrackers, but much, much scarier. So, we want this special power only for good things, like lights, right? **Did you know?** The sun is like a giant, natural nuclear power station! It makes all the heat and light that helps plants grow and keeps us warm.

Leaders Talking About Stopping Things

President Trump and his country are talking to Iran. They want Iran to promise something very important about their special power plans. It's like when your parents ask you to promise to stop doing something that might be a little bit naughty or dangerous. President Trump wants Iran to stop doing some things with their special power for a very long time. He said 20 years! Wow, 20 years is a super long time! Maybe even longer than your teachers have been teaching! For 20 whole years, they want Iran to stop making the super strong power in a way that could be dangerous. They want them to use it only for good things, like making electricity for homes.

What is "Uranium" and "Enrichment"?

This special power comes from something called "uranium." Uranium is a type of special rock or metal found deep in the ground. It's not like the rocks you find outside your house; it's very special! Now, "enrichment" is a big word too. It means making the uranium even stronger and more powerful. Imagine you have a tiny little seed. If you "enrich" it, you make it grow into a big, strong tree! When we talk about uranium, "enrichment" means making it super-duper strong. If it's made *too* strong, it could be used for those very big, scary explosions I told you about. **Did you know?** Uranium is a heavy metal, much heavier than iron or copper! It can even glow in the dark sometimes because of its special energy!

Why Stop Making it Stronger?

So, President Trump and other leaders want Iran to stop "enriching" uranium so much. They want them to promise to keep the uranium only strong enough to make electricity, and not strong enough to make scary big explosions. It's like having a toy that can do two things: it can make happy music, or it can make a loud, scary noise. The leaders want Iran to only make the happy music with their special power, and not the scary noise. They also want Iran to take away some of the special "nuclear fuel" they have. This is like taking away the super strong ingredients so you can't cook a dangerous meal. If they remove the fuel, they can't make the dangerous power easily.

A Promise for 20 Years

President Trump thinks that if Iran promises to stop doing these things for 20 years, it will be enough. It will give everyone a very long time to trust each other. It's a bit like playing a game with your friends. If someone breaks the rules, you might not want to play with them anymore. But if they promise to follow the rules for a very, very long time, you start to trust them again, right? This promise is called showing "real commitment." It means they truly mean what they say and will do it. **Did you know?** Many countries in the world have special rules and agreements to make sure nuclear power is used safely and only for good things.

Why is This Important for All of Us?

Even though these countries are far away, this news is important for everyone in the world, including us here in Africa. When leaders talk and agree to keep things safe, it helps make the whole world a more peaceful place. It means less chance of big problems and more chance for everyone to live happily and safely. We want all countries to use their special powers for good things – like making electricity, helping sick people, and building amazing new things. Not for big, scary explosions. Here in Africa, many of our countries use other kinds of wonderful power. We use the bright sun to make electricity (that's solar power!), and we use big rivers to make power (that's hydro power!). These are also amazing and safe ways to get energy. So, when you hear about leaders talking about big things like nuclear power, remember they are trying to keep our world safe and happy for all of us, especially for you! Keep shining bright, my friends! And stay curious about the world around you!
summary: President Trump from America wants Iran to promise to stop making dangerous special power for 20 years to keep the world safe.
whyMatters: This is important because leaders want to make sure powerful energy is only used for good things like electricity, not for big scary explosions, so everyone can live in peace.
